name: Advanced Encryption Standard (AES)
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: FIPS 197 (2001, updated 2023) standardizes the Advanced Encryption Standard — the universally-used symmetric block cipher (AES-128, AES-192, AES-256). Foundational cryptographic primitive.
relationToCloudSecurity: AES is the default symmetric cipher across every cloud platform and protocol.
relationToAISecurity: Used in AI model storage encryption, training data protection, and inference confidentiality.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).
